authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 21 is the primary U.S. public regulatory baseline for FAA certification procedures, production approvals, PMA requirements, quality-system obligations, manufacturing-facility changes, inspection and test limits, and conformity boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Production approval, quality-system, conformity, manufacturing-change, and release-boundary framing

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A is direct FAA guidance on production-approval quality systems, supplier control, approved supplier lists, traceability, supplier change control, supplier nonconformance reporting, direct shipment, conformity records, and PAH responsibilities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier control, change notification, traceability, nonconformance, and release-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG is the recognized steward for 9100-series aviation, space, and defense QMS standards. The public 9100 page anchors supply-chain quality, product integrity, schedule and cost performance, and good-practice QMS expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Aerospace and defense QMS prerequisite and terminology framing
used_for: product integrity and good-practice boundaries for coordination work

authoritative_sources.title: Deployment Support Materials
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG deployment support materials are developed under the IAQG Requirements Team to maintain aviation, space, and defense quality requirements and stakeholder communications, including supplier performance, product quality, and on-time delivery support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Supplier, product-quality, and on-time-delivery handoff framing
used_for: supplier performance and quality handoff expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG SCMH is a recognized aerospace supply-chain handbook used for supplier management and deployment support across aviation, space, and defense supply chains.

authoritative_sources.title: 9131 Nonconformance Data Definition and Documentation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG 9131 is a recognized aerospace standard page for uniform submittal of nonconformity information for notification or approval when contractually invoked at any level of aviation, space, and defense supply chains.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Nonconformance evidence packaging and approval-boundary escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 9145:2016 Guidance Presentation - APQP and PPAP Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG 9145 guidance publicly identifies aerospace APQP and PPAP requirements that shape production-readiness, supplier, and change-handoff artifacts before formal engineering or supplier approval ownership begins.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: APQP, PPAP, FAI-delta, and production-readiness handoff preparation

authoritative_sources.title: NASA Systems Engineering Handbook, Section 6.0 Crosscutting Technical Management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NASA's public systems engineering handbook describes configuration management, baseline control, change requests, CCB approval, impact assessment, routing to evaluators, controlled technical data, and communication of approved changes for aerospace programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Configuration-control, impact-assessment, CCB routing, and closure framing
used_for: impact assessment and evaluator routing expectations

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, prior owner, next owner candidate, program and facility context, part, drawing, specification, serial, lot, batch, unit, tail, effectivity, baseline, supplier, hold, release, and tenant authority rules before any handoff or closure claim.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Cross-team engineering-change coordination and routing
workflow_stages.description: Normalize aerospace change terminology and system evidence, reconcile CCB, engineering, quality, supplier, production, inventory, and logistics timing, and prepare handoff or blocker outputs with explicit artifact and acceptance requirements.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Traceability-aware configuration, nonconformance, and release-boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve baseline, effectivity, serial, lot, inventory, quality-hold, supplier-change, nonconformance, FAI, direct-ship, and release evidence when conflicts, holds, substitutions, or safety-sensitive cases appear so the lane escalates instead of guessing.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Closure confirmation and orchestrator return
workflow_stages.description: Mark transfer complete only when destination owner, required records, acceptance proof, and blocker resolution are explicit; otherwise return safely for escalation or human policy resolution.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: engineering change requests, engineering change orders, engineering change proposals, notice of change records, CCB agendas, CCB minutes, CCB action logs, and implementation plans
configuration item identifiers, baseline records, released drawings, specifications, bills of material, effectivity tables, redline packages, digital thread links, and PLM or PDM references
production orders, travelers, routers, work orders, unit, tail, serial, batch, lot, material, and heat identifiers
inventory states, quarantine or segregation records, warehouse and line-side locations, shortage logs, transfer records, shipment references, and traceability logs
quality holds, nonconformance reports, MRB records, deviation or waiver requests, FAI packages, source inspection evidence, conformity records, release records, and corrective-action packages

supplier records, approved supplier status, supplier NOCs, supplier nonconformance records, certificates of conformity, purchase order flowdowns, direct-ship authorization, and supplier corrective-action responses
handoff summaries, dependency trackers, blocker logs, routing confirmations, closure confirmations, configuration-control routing packages, and escalation notes

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for aerospace and defense engineering-change coordination
organize authoritative records, required artifacts, next owners, acceptance proof, and blocker states for engineering, quality, production, supplier, inventory, logistics, and CCB handoffs
summarize configuration, effectivity, traceability, inventory, supplier, release, and throughput dependencies without implying final execution or approval authority
escalate: ECO, ECP, NOC, CCB, MRB, engineering change, baseline, drawing, specification, effectivity, safety, airworthiness, export-control, classified-program, or legal decisions requiring formal authority

quality hold, deviation, waiver, release, FAI acceptance, conformity, supplier nonconformance, direct-ship, or customer notification decisions requiring formal quality, supplier, engineering, regulator, or customer authority
conflicting public sources, customer flowdowns, contract requirements, or tenant policy that materially change configuration-control, release, supplier, notification, or ownership handling
refuse: bypass traceability, configuration-control, supplier-control, quality, hold, release, airworthiness, export, or safety controls to preserve schedule or throughput
approve held, nonconforming, incorrectly configured, unreleased, or supplier-questioned product for build, shipment, use, or customer delivery
treat supplier change, supplier nonconformance, substitution approval, MRB disposition, or safety-release scope as coordination-only when formal approval is required
update released product data, move inventory, release work orders, notify suppliers as an accountable owner, or close CCB actions without destination-owner acceptance

domain_failure_modes: A handoff proceeds without authoritative ECO, ECP, NOC, baseline, effectivity, release, supplier, or destination-owner evidence.
Conflicting PLM, PDM, ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, and supplier records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of a traceability and configuration blocker.
Open NCR, MRB, deviation, waiver, or quality hold status is treated as a schedule nuisance rather than a formal release boundary.

Supplier process change, material substitution, source change, direct-ship, or supplier nonconformance is handled as routine coordination instead of routed to procurement, supplier quality, engineering, customer, or regulatory owners.
CCB, baseline, effectivity, or released drawing changes are treated as administrative updates instead of formal configuration-control decisions.
Safety-sensitive, airworthiness-adjacent, export-controlled, classified, or contract-flowdown scope is treated as a coordination detail rather than escalated for authorized review.
The lane absorbs engineering, quality, production, inventory, supplier, or logistics execution work instead of preserving coordination boundaries.
Closure is declared without proof that the right destination owner accepted the required artifact set.

workflow_questions.question: What batch, lot, inventory, or supplier records are authoritative?
workflow_questions.answer: Authoritative records are tenant-designated PLM, PDM, configuration-management, ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier-management, traceability, and customer or government data-system records for the affected program, part, drawing, serial, lot, batch, unit, tail, supplier, purchase order, hold, release, FAI, and CCB or MRB state. Public sources define categories and control expectations, but exact record primacy is tenant supplied.

workflow_questions.question: What release, hold, or traceability boundaries govern the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane preserves FAA production and conformity boundaries, IAQG QMS and supplier-control expectations, nonconformance notification or approval boundaries, configuration-control and CCB authority, and tenant hold or release rules. It can summarize blocker state but cannot release product, clear holds, approve use-as-is, alter baselines, or bypass traceability.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and formal quality, engineering, or supplier approval begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops when the next action requires engineering signoff, CCB approval, MRB disposition, quality release, FAI or conformity acceptance, supplier process change approval, supplier substitution approval, customer or regulator notification decision, legal or contract interpretation, export-control determination, safety-critical judgment, or direct execution.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Work is handed off without required context or artifacts
Multiple teams appear to own the same step
The lane is asked to absorb execution work instead of coordinating it
Ownership is ambiguous across multiple teams
A handoff is attempted without the required context

unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public FAA, IAQG, and NASA sources establish the need for formal configuration, nonconformance, supplier, and release control, but the controlling CCB charter, MRB authority matrix, delegated engineering authority, and customer approval path are tenant supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Customer, contract, export-control, and classified-program flowdowns
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources do not determine customer-specific, defense contract, ITAR/EAR, classified-program, CDRL, or government data-rights handling for a tenant. Those constraints must be retrieved and conflicts escalated before routing or closure.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific release and regulator-notification rules outside the U.S. FAA baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: The public FAA baseline anchors U.S. aviation production expectations, but non-U.S. civil aviation, military airworthiness, space-program, or customer-specific notification and release rules may be stricter and must be retrieved rather than assumed.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Plant-specific systems of record and owner matrices
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Public sources define categories of authoritative records, but the tenant truth set across PLM, PDM, ERP, MES, WMS, QMS, supplier portals, configuration-management tools, traceability systems, and government or customer portals must be resolved at runtime.
